result:
Done. Here's a summary of what was built:

## X/Twitter DM Channel Extension (`extensions/x-twitter/`)

**12 files created**, following the existing channel extension pattern (modeled after `extensions/msteams/`):

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin manifest (kind=channels) |
| `package.json` | Package metadata with channel config |
| `index.ts` | Plugin entry point, registers channel |
| `src/runtime.ts` | PluginRuntime singleton |
| `src/types.ts` | TypeScript types (credentials, config, account, DM event) |
| `src/token.ts` | Credential resolution (config + env vars) |
| `src/send.ts` | Send DMs via X API v2 with OAuth 1.0a signing |
| `src/probe.ts` | Health check via GET /2/users/me |
| `src/outbound.ts` | ChannelOutboundAdapter (text + media) |
| `src/onboarding.ts` | Interactive setup wizard for API credentials |
| `src/channel.ts` | Full ChannelPlugin implementation |
| `src/channel.test.ts` | **22 unit tests** covering all adapters |

**Key features:**
- OAuth 1.0a authentication for X API v2
- DM send via `POST /2/dm_conversations/with/:participant_id/messages`
- Probe via `GET /2/users/me` for health checking
- Onboarding flow with env var detection (`X_TWITTER_API_KEY`, etc.)
- DM policy support (open/pairing/allowlist)
- Target resolution for user IDs and @usernames
- 10,000 char chunk limit (X DM max)
- Zero TypeScript errors, all tests passing
